page: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 physical cores on the arm64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-V2 model, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system allocates dedicated CPU resources with 1.0 thread per core, supported by 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, which equates to 2.0 GB of memory per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, achieving top-tier results in both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations. Multi-threaded decompression and software compilation tests also place this server in the top tier. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Additionally, while prompt processing for medium and large language models is strong, text generation speeds are average to poor, highlighting a tradeoff in sequential generation tasks.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high physical core density, the c8g.metal-48xl offers high resource density for multi-threaded workloads. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a specialized choice for CPU-bound tasks. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving, where its massive parallel processing capabilities and top-tier memory bandwidth can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture. Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz, it is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds remain average. This server is highly efficient for parallelized, CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 192 arm64 physical cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-density, bare-metal arm64 server delivering exceptional multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads.

384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
